title: The Second Sunday of Advent
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## December 4, 2022 | Second Sunday of Advent

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Second Sunday of Advent falls within the penitential season of Advent, the four-week period preceding Christmas focused on preparation and repentance. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would likely include Isaiah 11:1-10 (the shoot from Jesse), Psalm 72:1-7, 18-19, Romans 15:4-13, and Matthew 3:1-12 (John the Baptist's call to repentance). The liturgical color is deep blue or purple, and the candles on the Advent wreath would include lighting of the second candle (traditionally representing peace or preparation).

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating post-General Convention (79th GC held in Baltimore in July 2022) implementation of several resolutions, including contentious decisions regarding human sexuality and marriage. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kimberly Chastain (elected in 2017), was managing ongoing membership and financial pressures affecting many mainline Protestant congregations. Saint John's, as the diocesan cathedral, would have been central to any major diocesan announcements or liturgical observances.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced mild weather for early December 2022, with temperatures in the 40s-50s Fahrenheit and minimal snowfall in the first week of the month. The Colorado legislature was in recess following the November 2022 midterm elections, in which Democrats retained control of both chambers and Democratic governor Jared Polis won re-election. Locally, downtown Denver and Capitol Hill (the cathedral's neighborhood) were preparing for holiday season events and shopping, with inflation and economic concerns dominating local discussions.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The nation was focused on the midterm election aftermath, with Republicans having gained control of the House of Representatives while Democrats retained the Senate, defying historical patterns. Elon Musk's chaotic takeover of Twitter (completed October 27) was entering its second month, marked by widespread staff layoffs and policy reversals causing significant cultural debate. The January 6 Select Committee was holding its final public hearings before releasing its report, dominating political news cycles in early December.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine, now in its tenth month, was characterized by brutal winter warfare and Russian losses mounting as Ukrainian forces mounted a counteroffensive in the Kharkiv region. The FIFA World Cup was underway in Qatar, with matches scheduled through mid-December, serving as a major global cultural focus. A massive winter storm system was impacting Europe, exacerbating energy crises and humanitarian concerns as nations struggled with Russian gas cutoffs.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture was entering peak holiday season consumption and nostalgia, with Christmas merchandise and decorations dominant in retail spaces despite ongoing inflation concerns affecting consumer spending. A sense of political exhaustion and division persisted after midterm elections, alongside deep partisan anxiety about the future. The cultural conversation was fractured between those seeking spiritual and communal renewal through the holiday season and those experiencing genuine economic anxiety and social disorientation about national direction.
